cases.name: mcp coding prompt
cases.description: Offline MCP context test for a realistic coding workflow.
cases.query: Refactor the parser helper and keep the pytest suite concise without breaking CLI compatibility.

history.content: We need to keep this repo offline-testable.
Yes, no API keys or network calls in the test path.
The CLI should stay stable while we add memory capture.
Agreed, preserve the public commands.
Prefer clear error messages and small diffs.
Small focused changes are easier to review.
We also want the benchmark to be offline.
The benchmark should use local or no-run mode.
Use pytest for validation.
pytest works well for this repo.

memories.content: Prefer pytest for backend and MCP tests.
Auth flows must stay offline-testable without API keys.
The repo has a TypeScript CLI front-end and a Python core engine.

cases.name: medium coding prompt
cases.description: Medium offline coding prompt with enough history to show token savings.
cases.query: Refactor the parser helper so it is easier to test, keep the pytest suite concise, preserve CLI compatibility, and make sure the MCP path stays offline-testable. Please give a concrete implementation plan, likely failure modes, and the tests you would add first.

history.content: We need to keep the repo offline-testable.
Right, no API keys or network calls in the test path.
The CLI should stay stable while we add memory capture.
Agreed, preserve the public commands.
Prefer clear error messages and small diffs.
Small focused changes are easier to review.
We also want the benchmark to be offline.
The benchmark should use local or no-run mode.
Use pytest for validation.
pytest works well for this repo.
Prompt injection text should never become a durable memory.
Filter it before capture and keep memory notes untrusted.

memories.content: Prefer pytest for backend and MCP tests.

The repo has a TypeScript CLI front-end and a Python core engine.
